serving.description: 1 serving (approximately 1 roll with sauce and yogurt)
serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Beyti Kebab is approximately 250 g, comprising one or two minced meat rolls wrapped in lavash bread, topped with tomato sauce and accompanied by a yogurt dip.

nutrient_source.title: Beyti Kebab — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Beyti Kebab (cr_id G00010175). All per-100g macros and micronutrients are taken from this record with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central ground lamb/beef composite (USDA SR Legacy #13627 and #23557) as HPB does not report saturated fat breakdown; documented as adapted.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; analogous estimate based on mixed meat-and-bread dish with low carbohydrate content
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Beyti Kebab. GI estimated at 45 (low) based on analogous mixed meat-and-flatbread dishes from the University of Sydney GI Database. The dish is predominantly fat and protein (fat 26.4 g, protein 10.7 g per 100 g) with only 5.24 g carbohydrate per 100 g, which strongly attenuates glycemic response. Lavash bread component contributes refined carbohydrate but is a minor fraction of the dish. GL per 250 g serving = (45 × 5.24 × 2.5) / 100 = 5.9 g, classified low. Estimation is conservative and defensible.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 1080 mg per 100 g — far exceeds the 600 mg/100 g upper threshold for hypertension-safe foods
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit Beyti Kebab due to its extremely high sodium content of 1080 mg/100 g; if consumed, request reduced-salt preparation and limit portion to no more than 100 g, pairing with potassium-rich vegetables to partially offset sodium load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low carbohydrate (5.24 g/100 g) and low GI (~45) are favourable, but high fat content (26.4 g/100 g) contributes to caloric density and insulin resistance risk
type2_diabetes.guidance: Beyti Kebab has a low glycemic impact due to minimal carbohydrate and low GI, making it broadly suitable for blood glucose management in Type 2 Diabetes; however, portion size should be controlled to limit total fat and calorie intake, and the lavash bread component should be minimised where possible.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High total fat (26.4 g/100 g), estimated saturated fat ~10.5 g/100 g, cholesterol 85 mg/100 g, and sodium 1080 mg/100 g collectively elevate cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume Beyti Kebab only occasionally and in small portions, given its high saturated fat, cholesterol, and sodium content, all of which are established risk factors for atherosclerosis and hypertensive cardiovascular events.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Red meat (arachidonic acid source), high sodium (1080 mg/100 g), estimated saturated fat ~10.5 g/100 g, and nightshade compounds in tomato sauce are all pro-inflammatory
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should limit Beyti Kebab due to its red meat content (a primary arachidonic acid source that amplifies inflammatory cytokines), high sodium contributing to tissue swelling, and tomato sauce containing nightshade compounds that may worsen symptoms in sensitive individuals.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Beyti Kebab is a Turkish grilled minced meat dish consisting of seasoned ground lamb or beef (sometimes a blend) shaped into a log, grilled on a skewer, then wrapped tightly in lavash or thin flatbread. The roll is sliced into rounds, arranged on a plate, and topped with a homemade tomato-based sauce enriched with butter and spices. It is traditionally served with a generous yogurt dip on the side. The dish originates from Turkey and is named after the Istanbul restaurant Beyti, which popularised it in the mid-20th century. In Singapore, it appears on Middle Eastern and Turkish restaurant menus as a main course for lunch or dinner. Nutritionally, Beyti Kebab is energy-dense at 302 kcal per 100 g, driven primarily by its high fat content of 26.4 g/100 g (estimated saturated fat ~10.5 g/100 g) and moderate protein of 10.7 g/100 g. Carbohydrate is low at 5.24 g/100 g, yielding a low glycemic index (~45) and low glycemic load per serving (~5.9). Sodium is very high at 1080 mg/100 g, making it a significant concern for individuals with hypertension or cardiovascular disease. Cholesterol is 85 mg/100 g. The dish is halal-suitable when prepared with permissible meat. It is not suitable for vegetarians or vegans. Allergens include gluten (flatbread) and dairy (yogurt, butter). Clinically, the low carbohydrate profile is favourable for blood glucose management, but the high fat, saturated fat, cholesterol, and sodium content require portion control for individuals managing hypertension, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Beyti Kebab (cr_id G00010175) with stated confidence 0.95. Saturated fat is an adapted estimate from USDA SR Legacy ground meat composites and documented. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database framework, clearly documented.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (10.5 g/100 g) is an adapted estimate derived from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for ground lamb (#13627) and ground beef (#23557), proportioned to the HPB fat total of 26.4 g/100 g. HPB does not report saturated fat breakdown for this entry. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, full vitamin panel, phosphorus, zinc, selenium, and moisture data are not reported in the HPB Foodo entry for Beyti Kebab and are not available in a directly matching approved-source record as of 2026-04-16. 3. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 as reported by HPB; this is plausible given the minimal bread and vegetable content in the standard preparation. 4. GI of 45 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for Beyti Kebab was found in the University of Sydney GI Database or peer-reviewed literature as of 2026-04-16. 5. Beyti Kebab is a Middle Eastern / Turkish dish served in Singapore; multilingual names provided in Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi to support Singapore-context retrieval, consistent with G6 Singaporean-context multilingual rules applied to non-Singaporean dishes served locally.
